findings.message: No model declared in frontmatter; omitting model leaves tier selection to the runtime, which may allocate an inappropriate model for the task
No tools declared; body instructs writing Markdown files ('Save as Markdown in specified folder') and performing web research, requiring at minimum Write and WebSearch
No model declared in frontmatter; omitting model leaves tier selection to the runtime
No tools declared; body instructs generating frontend-design-spec.md and using web search for best practices, requiring at minimum Write and WebSearch
No model declared in frontmatter; omitting model leaves tier selection to the runtime
Task declared in tools on line 4 but body on line 14 explicitly states 'You are not responsible or allowed to create tasks or actions'
No model declared in frontmatter; omitting model leaves tier selection to the runtime
Task declared on line 4 but body on line 14 explicitly prohibits task creation; no workflow step exercises this tool
'relevant subheadings' on line 22 lacks measurable criteria for what makes a subheading relevant in this context
No model declared in frontmatter; omitting model leaves tier selection to the runtime
NotebookEdit declared on line 4 but the agent only writes a Markdown plan.md file; Jupyter notebook editing is never part of the workflow
'relevant technical details' on line 98 is unmeasurable without specifying which details from the PRD qualify
No model declared in frontmatter; omitting model leaves tier selection to the runtime
Edit declared on line 4 but body describes remediation as a checklist in the report, not as implemented code fixes; no workflow step calls Edit
MultiEdit declared on line 4 but body never implements code changes; same reasoning as Edit
NotebookEdit declared on line 4 but agent only writes security-report.md; Jupyter notebook editing is never needed for a security audit
'suggest an appropriate location first' on line 25 provides no measurable criteria for what makes a location appropriate
'References to relevant security standards or best practices' on line 30 does not specify which standards apply (OWASP, CWE, NIST, etc.)
Core codebase review tools Read, Grep, LS absent from tools list; Bash can substitute but makes systematic code discovery less ergonomic
No tools declared; body describes building complete working applications and iterating on code, requiring at minimum Edit, Write, Bash, Read
No model declared in frontmatter; omitting model leaves tier selection to the runtime
Body describes a 5-step development process but never specifies what files are produced, in what format, or where they are written; callers have no way to predict output
